The installed SB3 version likely uses string policy name instead of explicit class import. Analysis: The policy string 'MultiInputPolicy' is valid in newer SB3 versions, and we don't need to import the class. Decision: Remove imports of MultiInputPolicy, use 'MultiInputPolicy' string in PPO constructor. Also fix make_env to not reset inside env_fn (let SubprocVecEnv handle seeding). trajectory.description: Observation: Previous training crashed due to CNN input shape mismatch. Error: expected input[40,7,3,7] to have 3 channels, but got 7 channels. Analysis: SB3's MultiInputPolicy automatically applies VecTransposeImage, which transposes image from HWC to CHW. Our manual permute(0,3,1,2) further transposed, resulting in (B,7,7,3) -> (B,7,3,7) after VecTransposeImage? Actually the chain caused 7 channels. Fix: remove permute, assume image already (B,C,H,W). Also, MultiInputPolicy expects only 'image' and 'vector' keys, not custom keys like 'direction' and 'mission_tokens'. So I'll merge direction and tokenized mission into a single 'vector' observation. Decision: Update model.py (MissionWrapper to output vector, feature extractor to split vector), predict.py to match, and train.py to use 200k timesteps for a fast baseline. Then submit eval immediately.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed at first rollout end in MetricsCallback. Error: KeyError: 0 when accessing ep_info_buffer items. Analysis: SB3's VecMonitor stores episode info as dict with keys 'r' (reward), 'l' (length), 't' (time), not tuples. Trying to index with [0] fails. The training itself proceeded past environment creation and started collecting rollouts, so the core pipeline is functional - just the logging callback needs fixing. Decision: Fix callback to use ep['r'] for episode reward from VecMonitor dict. Keep everything else the same (200k timesteps, same hyperparameters) and rerun. Then submit eval immediately after training completes.

trajectory.description: Observation: First baseline training (200k timesteps) yielded 0.0 success_rate across all envs, with mean reward 0.0. The training progressed (explained_variance improved to 0.643), but policy failed any success. Analysis: PPO on Minigrid with sparse rewards needs substantially more environment interactions to learn meaningful behaviors. Multi-task across 10 diverse environments is challenging. Decision: Increase training timesteps to 5M (≈60-70 minutes). Keep same architecture but increase network width (256-dim hidden layers) for more capacity. Submit eval after training to measure improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 5 (5M timesteps multi-task PPO) yielded 0.0 success_rate (actually 0.002). Training curve shows mean episodic reward ~0.05-0.1, almost no success episodes. Analysis: Multi-task PPO across 10 diverse Minigrid environments with sparse rewards fails to learn meaningful behaviors. The policy needs to solve fundamentally different tasks (navigation, key-door, lava crossing) using a single set of parameters, causing interference and weak gradients. Additionally, partial observability (7x7 grid) likely benefits from memory. Decision: Switch to separate policies per environment using RecurrentPPO with LSTM for memory. Train each environment for 2M timesteps (~22 min each, total ~220 min), update predict.py to load per-environment models and manage LSTM hidden states. Execute all trainings sequentially in one iteration to save iterations, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Iteration 6 attempted to import RecurrentPPO but it's not available in base SB3. RecurrentPPO would require sb3-contrib and custom Dict policies, which adds complexity. Given baseline PPO multi-task (5M steps) gave near-zero success rate, likely due to task interference and insufficient steps per task. Decision: switch to per-environment PPO models without recurrence. Train each environment separately for 500k steps (5M total, same as before but with dedicated models). This should reduce interference. Update predict.py to load per-env models (remove RecurrentPPO dependency). Train for 5M steps across all envs, submit eval. If still poor, will consider recurrence and longer training per env later.

trajectory.description: Observation: Per-env PPO with 500k steps each (iter 7) gave 0.002 success, nearly random. Training curves show occasional tiny rewards but no sustained learning. Analysis: Sparse reward Minigrid tasks require far more environment interactions and possibly architecture improvements. The current CNN (3 layers, 64 output) is small and mission embedding (32 dims) is weak. Decision: Increase model capacity (wider CNN, larger feature dim), increase parallel envs to 8 per task, train with 1M steps per env (10M total). Also raise n_steps=256, batch_size=128 for efficient updates. Run all training sequentially, then submit eval. This should take ~2.5h, leaving enough time for subsequent iterations if improvement is visible.

trajectory.description: Observation: Per-env PPO with 1M steps (iter 8) gave 0% success rate. Training curves show only small episodic rewards (<0.06), no success episodes. Analysis: Sparse rewards + partial observability (7×7 egocentric view) prevent PPO from discovering goal regions. PPO on-policy w/o memory fails on hard navigation tasks. Decision: Switch to DQN (off-policy, more sample‑efficient) with frame‑stacking (4 frames) to provide short‑term history and motion cues. Train 300k steps per env (≈50 min total), submit eval to test improvement. If positive, scale up later.

trajectory.description: Observation: DQN training crashed with channel mismatch. The image shape is HWC (7,7,12) but CNN expects CHW. SB3's VecTransposeDict apparently didn't transpose. Analysis: We can add a conditional permute in the feature extractor to handle both HWC and CHW input robustly. Decision: Fix model.py with shape-aware permute, clean up callback duplication, then rerun DQN training (300k per env). Submit eval after training to get first DQN baseline.

trajectory.description: Current PPO per env (500k steps each, frame stacking, intrinsic bonus) yields only 0.4% success. Need stronger exploration and more environment interactions. Switching to flat observation (flattening stacked image + direction + mission tokens) and using MLP instead of CNN – faster training and may extract better features for small 7×7 grids. Increase intrinsic bonus scale to 1.0, entropy coefficient to 0.05, and total steps per env to 1M (10M total). This training should take ~1.5h, leaving time for a possible final improvement iteration. Submit eval immediately after training.
